original_image_tags: A map of North America, rotated so East is roughly down. The map shows the outline of the United States, parts of Canada, Mexico, and the Caribbean. A large, dense cloud of black dots covers much of the central and eastern United States, extending into southern Canada and the Gulf of Mexico. The dot cloud represents simulated impact points.

elements.description: A map showing an impact footprint. The footprint is a dense cloud of dots resembling a circular or fan shape, overlaid on a coastline outline. Inside the footprint are several numbered labels: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 10. Below the footprint is text: 'Atlas IIAS Impacts', 'Random-Attitude Failure at 130 sec.', 'Thrust to 280 sec.', 'Breakup q-alpha = 5,000 deg-lb/ft²'. Below the map frame is the caption 'Figure 8. Atlas IIAS Impacts with Breakup'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content (a map of North America/US coastline with a dense cloud of dots representing an impact footprint), but Description B adds specific details (numbered labels, specific caption text) that Description A omits without contradiction.
